title: Add Distinct CSS Classes to Metrics Analyst Feature Buttons for Analytics Tracking
description:
Each 'More Info' button on the Metrics Analyst summary cards needs a unique CSS class or ID so they can be individually tracked via Pendo analytics. This allows product management to measure which cards users interact with most.

The six buttons already had unique classes:
1. `show_processed_dashboards`
2. `show_processed_reports`
3. `show_dashboard_widgets`
4. `show_suggested_metrics`
5. `show_ignored_facts`
6. `show_suggested_categories`

Any feature button or clickable element we want to track should have a unique class, even if no CSS is applied.
acceptance_criteria: Each 'More Info' button has a unique, descriptive CSS class
Classes follow a consistent naming convention
Pendo can target each button individually for analytics tracking
implementation_notes: The six unique classes were already present. Ryan confirmed he would configure Pendo targeting based on the existing class names.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed the unique classes were already in place. Ryan noted he would update Pendo accordingly and marked it complete.
